name: Mom's Chicken & Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the chicken in a pressure cooker with plenty of water, salt, and MSG (omit MSG if not desired). Cover the chicken with water, close the lid, place a 15-pound weight on top, and cook over medium-high heat.
2. When the steam releases 2 to 3 times per minute, reduce heat to low and continue cooking for an additional 15 minutes. Remove from heat and allow the chicken to cool before deboning it.
3. In a large bowl, mix flour, salt, MSG, and baking powder.
4. Beat eggs with about one-third of the cooled chicken stock (chilled) in another bowl. Gradually stir the egg mixture into the flour mixture until just combined.
5. On a floured surface, knead the dough to form a smooth ball. Roll out the dough until it is thinner than a nickel.
6. Cut the dough into dumpling-sized pieces (approximately 2 inches wide by 10 inches long).
7. In a large pot, bring the remaining chicken stock to a boil over medium-high heat. Add the chunks of deboned chicken and dumplings alternately, pushing each piece below the surface as soon as it is added.
8. Do not fold the dumplings; maintain constant boiling throughout cooking.
9. Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 12-15 minutes without lifting the lid during the first 10 minutes of cooking.
10. If necessary, add additional stock or pepper to taste before serving.
